title: SERINGAPATAM TROPHY
tagline: A SYMBOL OF VICTORY AND HISTORY
history: The trophy was instituted to commemorate the victory of the British over Tipu Sultan on 04 Nov 1799.
The trophy is made of sterling silver and depicts a Lion (the British) subduing the Tiger (Tipu Sultan).
The word 'Seringapatam' is inscribed on the front side of the trophy and the date 'IV May MDCCC' on the rear side.
On the sides it bears the two faces of the medal depicting the Lion subduing the Tiger.

On one side of the medal is the depiction of the storming of the Seringapatam Fort from an actual drawing made on the spot. On the other side is the meridian sun denoting the time of the assault, with a Persian inscription underneath.

title: NON COMMISSIONED OFFRS CUP

facts.Description: A regimental cup recording company achievements.

history: The Non Commissioned Offrs Cup is an alloy silver cup presented in 1894 to the officers of the 26th Regiment (Madras Infantry) by the NCOs of the Corps.
The names of the companies, their positions, and the years in which the trophy was won by them are inscribed on the reverse side of the trophy.

title: SIRA CUP
history: The Sira Cup, known as The Ohdewala Stakes - 1913, is a silver cup presented to the 11th Battalion by the First KGO Battalion Madras.

title: THE ANDIPATTI CHALLENGE CUP

facts.Presented By: Diwan Bahadur Pethachi Chettiar, Zamindar of Andipatti
facts.Description: Awarded to the best platoon in drill.

history: The Andipatti Challenge Cup is a silver cup presented to the First Territorial Battalion, 73rd Carnatic Infantry, by Diwan Bahadur Pethachi Chettiar, the Zamindar of Andipatti, in June 1922.
The cup was awarded for the best platoon in drill as part of an inter-platoon competition.

title: PLATOON CHALLENGE CUP

facts.Presented By: Honourable Ministers of the First Ministry

history: The Platoon Challenge Cup is a silver trophy presented to the 11th Battalion, 3rd Madras Regiment, by the Honourable Ministers of the First Ministry in 1923.

title: VENKATAGIRI SHIELD

facts.Presented By: Captain Sir The Rajah of Venkatgiri
facts.Description: An athletics shield depicting runners crossing the finishing line.

history: The Venkatagiri Shield was presented by Captain Sir The Rajah of Venkatgiri in 1923 to the 11th Battalion, Third Madras Regiment.
The shield depicts athletes running across the finishing line.
It is used as an inter-company athletics shield.

title: DOUBLE COMPANY HOCKEY CHALLENGE CUP

facts.Presented By: Capt WO Reid, Capt ACA Thackwell, Capt GW Condiran and Capt C Alexander
facts.Description: Presented by officers on their promotion to the next higher rank.

history: The Double Company Hockey Challenge Cup was presented to the 81st Pioneers by Captain WO Reid, Captain ACA Thackwell, Captain GW Condiran and Captain C Alexander.
The trophy was presented by the officers on their promotion to the next higher rank.
It is used for inter-company hockey competitions.

title: BOBBILI SHIELD

facts.Description: Awarded to the winning company in the Inter Coy SA Firing Competition.

history: The Bobbili Shield is a silver shield presented by the King of Bobbili, GCE, to the 11th Battalion, Third Madras Regiment in 1928.
The crest of the Bobbili Kingdom is depicted at the top of the shield.
The trophy was awarded to the winning company in the Inter Coy SA Firing Competition.

title: CHINAR SHIELD

facts.Presented By: Mohammed Sheikh Abdullah, Chief Minister of Jammu & Kashmir
facts.Description: Presented in recognition of the unit's commendable performance in Jammu & Kashmir during 1948.

history: The Chinar Shield was presented by Mohammed Sheikh Abdullah, Chief Minister of Jammu & Kashmir, in recognition of the commendable performance of the unit in Jammu & Kashmir during 1948.
